title: Tiruchi Means a Turning-Point Conference, Does It Not? (5)
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The Tamil reading copy repairs only source-confirmed or unambiguous OCR errors and page-break fractures. The canonical Tamil chapter remains unchanged. Kalaignar's 1970 address after Anna's death is retained at full length, including its repetition and direct forms of address.
paragraphs: Did I not explain in detail last week the highly successful Virugambakkam conference conducted while Anna was with us? After Anna left us, the fifth Tiruchi District DMK Conference was held with distinction here in Tiruchi itself in February 1970. This is how I began my speech at that conference!

"Beloved eyes of the movement! Dear mothers! Elders! Perarignar Anna... I ought to have begun my speech with those words of address. Instead, I stand before you today searching for that Anna and asking: Where is he? Where?
"Navalar, who came running when you summoned and commanded him, 'Come, younger brother! Come and take the chair!' is seated here. Where have you, who summoned and commanded him, gone?

"Anna, you would often praise Mathiazhagan, saying, 'He is always very stubborn, but he never fails to captivate us with his words, draw us to his side and get what he wants.' That stubborn Mathiazhagan is seated here. Leaving him behind, where have you gone, Anna?

"Anna, you would laugh and say, 'When Chitrarasu speaks, I shake with laughter, younger brother!' When that Chitrarasu made all of us shake with laughter here, where did you go without staying to enjoy it, Anna?

"N. V. N. would sit beside you, fold a betel leaf and hand it to you; you would take it, smiling in triumph, and tuck it into your mouth. Anna! N. V. N. is here to fold and offer the betel leaf. Our Anna who gave us victories, where have you gone?
"Anna, you would praise Anbazhagan for his nature: thinking deeply, remaining calm, and arguing with firmness. That Professor is here. Our glorious Anna, where have you gone?

"'What is it, Muthu? Are you coming in anger? Sit down!' you would say with a smile. Forgetting all his anger, he would sit before you with a cooled and peaceful face. That Madurai Muthu is at this conference. But Anna, who would cool his anger, where have you gone?
"'Mannai, it is Karunanidhi who tells me not to come to Mannargudi; it is not my fault, my boy,' you would tease. Mannai Narayanasamy, who bore that mischief, is here. Anna, where have you gone?
"'Anbil! Show me your eyes a little,' you would say, delighting in the sight of those round eyes. Our Anbil is here to show you those eyes. Anna, closing your own eyes, where have you gone?

"At Chidambaram, you sent me to the Kallakudi agitation with the words, 'Go, younger brother; return victorious, younger brother.' Anna, I went and I have returned. But where have you, who told me to return victorious, gone? Where have you gone? It is only after asking this that I address Perarignar Anna at this conference.

"I have faith. All of us gathered here have faith. Anna, you are right here. You are listening to all this speech. You see this surging enthusiasm. You see your dear younger sisters gathered here. You see the battalions of younger brothers. Anna, who kisses the edge of the flower-like lips of little children, you have gone nowhere. You are here! You are here! You live in our hearts, Anna! You are right here!"

At every conference, I would come to the stage in my capacity as the movement's Treasurer to submit all the accounts. Anna would ask how much had been collected. I would tell him that I could not reveal it, that it was a "suspense." He would joke, "Just tell me; I will not tell anyone outside." P. U. Shanmugam, standing nearby, would disclose the amount to him, and Anna would tease me: "See? See? Your own friend has told me!" I had watched and delighted in that beautiful portrait of Anna standing and speaking on the stage. To stand in that place and speak that day made my entire body tremble, my whole heart convulse, and my eyes turn into pools of tears! My Anna left me with a grief I had not felt even when I lost my mother or my father, a grief that will remain with me until I enter the grave! Golden ones of the movement, who placed this great responsibility upon my shoulders after Anna! I sit here trusting all of you. Each one of you appears to me as Anna. The comfort one elder brother should have given me, the counsel he should have offered, is now being given to me by you, the four crore people of the great Tamil family."

That is how, with tears, grief and a heavy heart, I began my speech at that Tiruchi District Conference. Then, on 24 January 1971, the DMK's special election conference was held at Ashok Nagar in Chennai. Until just before I spoke, we had expected an electoral alliance with the Indira Congress. When the talks failed and that party announced its candidates, I too released the DMK candidate list during my speech. But around midnight, after Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and G. Parthasarathi spoke from Delhi, it was decided that the Congress would not contest Assembly constituencies and would contest only ten parliamentary constituencies. In that election, the DMK won 184 seats; its allies won eight for the Communist Party of India, seven for the Forward Bloc, four for the Praja Socialist Party, six for the Indian Union Muslim League, and one for the Tamil Arasu Kazhagam. The DMK's victory was an immense historic achievement unmatched by any party in a Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly election until then.

Before the DMK's fifth general State conference was held in December 1975, the DMK State Women's Conference took place on 15 and 16 November that year at the Tamukkam Grounds in Madurai, in the Vetriselvi Anbazhagan pavilion and Saraswathi Kandasamy hall, under the chairmanship of Rani Anna, the wife of Perarignar Anna. Porselvi Ilamurugu inaugurated the conference. E. V. R. Maniammai unveiled the portrait of the elder Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar, and Poongothai unveiled the portrait of Dr Dharmambal. Alamelu Appadurai chaired the reception committee; Tamilarasi, MLC, was conference Treasurer; Devaki Pandian, Sakthi Kothandam, Amudha Raghavan and S. P. Sarguna were conference secretaries; and Madurai Anusuya headed the volunteer corps.

Following the Women's Conference, the DMK's fifth general State conference was held under my chairmanship in Coimbatore during the final week of December 1975. General Secretary Navalar inaugurated it, and Professor Anbazhagan unveiled the portraits of the movement's martyrs. The conference included "Victory Is Ours," a play featuring younger brother M. K. Stalin; "Chandramohan," featuring idealist actor S. S. R.; and "He Is Our God," performed by the movement's speakers. Thus, conferences have always had a long cultural heritage within the DMK.

For the tenth State conference to be held in Tiruchi this February, the site was selected a month and a half in advance, and work on the pavilion began and continues. I have already written about the movement's Treasurer personally inspecting the conference work. Since then, you will have seen in our newspapers the photographs of district secretaries from around Tiruchi visiting the site each day, inspecting the work and offering wholehearted cooperation. Even the commander of the Thanjavur District DMK, former minister and younger brother Ko. Si. Mani, who is unable to walk, came with the help of others to see the conference pavilion.

Such is his irrepressible enthusiasm! Likewise, other district secretaries have visited in great excitement: in particular younger brother I. Periasamy of Dindigul; the "fierce devotee," younger brother N. Periasamy of Thoothukudi; younger brother Pongalur Palanisamy of Coimbatore; younger brother M. R. K. Panneerselvam of Cuddalore; younger brother Moorthy of Madurai; and younger brother L. Mookiah of Theni. Along with them came L. Ganesan, Aranthangi Rajan, Sedapatti Muthiah, Ar. Sakkarapani, Kuthalam Kalyanam and Se. Ramalingam. Welcoming them, younger brother Nehru, Tiruchi City DMK Secretary M. Anbazhagan, Anbil Periasamy, Kajamalai Vijay, R. C. Ganesan, Kudamurutti Sekar, Mathivanan, Ko. Thangamani, K. N. Sekaran, Durai Kandasamy, N. T. R. Singaram, Durai, Navalpattu Viji, Thiagarajan, Raghupathi, Kennedy, Bhaskar, Mohan and numerous movement comrades took them around the pavilion and received them with delight.

I personally wrote to the leaders of all our allied parties inviting them to grace the conference with their participation. Treasurer and younger brother M. K. Stalin delivered the letters in person, and the party leaders have replied that they will certainly attend.

To compile all the resolutions to be adopted at the conference, Resolutions Committee Chairman and younger brother Pon. Muthuramalingam has written to every district secretary and learned about local issues. With the assistance of committee members Kuzhanthai Tamilarasan, Pudukkottai C. Muthusamy, Mee. A. Vaithilingam, Coimbatore K. R. Subbaiah, V. Sathyamoorthy and Adi Shankar, and in consultation with me, the committee is systematically preparing all the resolutions to be adopted.

A meeting of district organisers from the Women's Wing, Women's Volunteer Wing and Propaganda Committee was held on 20 January 2014 at Kalaignar Arivalayam in Tiruchi, under the chairmanship of younger brother K. N. Nehru and in the presence of Deputy General Secretary S. P. Sarguna Pandian and Women's Wing President Noorjahan Begum. They adopted a resolution concerning the participation of Women's Wing members from across Tamil Nadu. State Women's Wing Secretary Pudukkottai Vijaya, State Women's Volunteer Wing Secretary Karl Marx, Kanchana Kamalanathan, Vijaya Thayanban, Tiruppur Sulakshana, Tiruvarur Vijayakumari, Bhavani Rajendran, Vasanthi Stanley, Sankari Narayanan, Nalini Sarangam and Chithiramughi Sathyavani Muthu participated.

District secretaries who meet me in Chennai proudly tell me how many buses and vans from their districts have paid advances and when they will depart. Meanwhile, the Rajya Sabha election and the work for the general parliamentary election have arrived as well. We must give those tasks the attention they require.

Conducting the conference successfully requires substantial funds. The party headquarters has therefore printed "ticket" books under proper procedure and accounting and handed them to the reception committee chairman. He has distributed the tickets among union and town units. You will have seen in Murasoli the photographs of the first instalments contributed: fifteen lakh rupees from the Thoothukudi District DMK; twenty-five lakh from the Ariyalur and Perambalur District units; twenty-five lakh from Tiruvannamalai District; ten lakh from Karur District; five lakh from the BHEL Labour Progressive Federation; one lakh from the Tiruchi Regional Labour Progressive Federation of the Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation; and five lakh from the Madurai District DMK. Those preparing to collect and contribute funds on behalf of other organisations must recognise that the conference date is approaching and come forward to present them.

Meanwhile, as though the work done by our party and our allies were not enough, the ruling party of Tamil Nadu has come forward to do conference work for us in its own style! Does that sound confusing? Our party workers will rise with still greater energy only if they are provoked. As if to help them do so, the police themselves, acting on instructions from somewhere, removed all the flex boards we had erected in Tiruchi for this conference. Even though the courts have permitted parties to erect flex boards before such events and remove them afterwards, the AIADMK government's police removed all our conference boards before the conference had even taken place. At the same time, they left untouched the AIADMK birthday banners for Chief Minister Jayalalithaa placed right beside the DMK boards. Photographs of both scenes have been published in Murasoli. As the conference draws nearer, what other kinds of "sacred service" will they perform? Our movement grew by overcoming countless obstacles of this kind!

They imagine that "if the comb is hidden, the wedding itself will stop"! But as far as the DMK is concerned, there is not the slightest doubt that it will cast aside all such opposition and that the Tiruchi conference will once again secure a distinguished place in the movement's history. My dear Udanpirappugal, bulls who, when told to cut and bring, return having bundled and brought it all; comrades who turn every movement conference into a victory conference and immerse the leadership in a sea of joy: set forth now to inscribe the tenth State conference in golden letters upon the list of victorious conferences!
The tenth State conference,
Has the world ever seen another like it?
Conduct it so magnificently that the nation marvels in those words. Waiting each day with that confidence is your loving elder brother, your Udanpirappu!
With affection,
M. K.
26 January 2014
